reading-inserted-results.label: Reading inserted results
reading-inserted-results.summary: Learn how to read and verify the results of INSERT operations in SQL, including how to check what was added and understand the impact of your statements on the inventory_items table.

ex_1.title: Insert and Read Back a Row

ex-1.prompt: Insert a new row into the inventory_items table with the following values: name = 'Desk Organizer', category = 'Accessories', price = 13.50. Then, write a SELECT statement to show the row(s) where name is 'Desk Organizer'.

ex-3.title: Verify Multiple Inserts
ex-3.prompt: Insert two new rows into inventory_items: (name = 'Mini Poster', category = 'Decor', price = 6.00) and (name = 'Travel Cup', category = 'Kitchen', price = 8.75). Then, select all rows where category is 'Kitchen'.

ex-4.title: What Can You Learn by Selecting After Insert?
ex-4.prompt: After inserting a new row and running a SELECT query for it, which of the following can you confirm? Choose all that apply.
ex-4.hint: Think about what information is visible in the selected row.

help.concept: Selecting after an insert lets you verify the new row's presence and see how the database filled in any default or auto-generated values.
help.hint_1: You can check both your inserted values and any defaults the database applied.
help.hint_2: You cannot see the exact SQL you ran, but you can see the resulting data.

options.a: a. That the row was actually added
options.b: b. The values of columns you inserted
options.c: c. The default values for columns you did not specify
options.d: d. The SQL statement you used for the insert

ex-5.title: Default Value for status
ex-5.prompt: When you insert a row into inventory_items and do not specify the status column, what value will status have by default?
ex-5.hint: Check the table definition for the default value of status.

help.concept: Columns with a DEFAULT value in the schema will use that value if not specified in an INSERT.
help.hint_1: Look for the DEFAULT keyword in the inventory_items schema for status.
help.hint_2: The default is a string value set in the table definition.

ex-5.template: The default value for status is [blank].

sketch-1.title: How to Check Inserted Data
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
After you run an `INSERT` statement, you can confirm the new row by running a `SELECT` query. For example, if you insert a new item into the `inventory_items` table, you can use:

```sql
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE name = 'New Item';
```

This will show you the row(s) matching the name you just inserted. Checking inserted results helps you verify that your data was added as expected.

sketch-2.title: Default Values After Insert
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown: Some columns in `inventory_items` have default values, such as `status` (default: 'draft') and `last_updated` (default: '2026-02-01'). If you omit these columns in your `INSERT` statement, the database will automatically fill them with their default values. You can see these defaults by selecting the new row after insertion.
